spec_match.rationale: The page now runs a real SPSC ring buffer with producer and consumer workers sharing a SharedArrayBuffer, and the paused run calls Atomics.pause inside the consumer's empty-spin path.

interactive.rationale: Users can tune message count, ring capacity, producer work, and consumer work, then run tight-spin and paused worker benchmarks with live metrics and ring-state visualization.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The prose now matches the implementation: two workers, a SAB-backed ring, real empty-probe counts, and an honest fallback when isolation is unavailable.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo covers isolation failure, missing Atomics.pause, full queue checks, empty consumer probes, and configurable producer/consumer speed imbalance.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5106098833719296 link and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: The page uses design-system variables for colors, sets button text colors with button backgrounds, and avoids inline event handlers.

summary: The former main-thread simulator was replaced with a real cross-origin-isolated SPSC worker benchmark. The concept now measures actual SharedArrayBuffer producer/consumer runs, reports the effect of Atomics.pause on empty spins, and clearly labels non-isolated fallback behavior.
